Three notification and cleanup options from #56: one summary per scheduled run instead of dozens of messages, a heads-up when a container is auto-updated, and optional pruning of the superseded image.

## Added

- **Summarise scheduled runs** (Settings → Notifications). A scheduled run of many containers/VMs used to send one message per item on every channel — 45 containers meant 45 emails. BombVault can now send a single "N of M succeeded" summary per run instead. Healthchecks was already summarised; this brings the message channels (webhook, Matrix, email, ntfy, Unraid) in line. Off by default, and manual backups still notify per item. (#56)
- **Notify on container update.** When "update after backup" upgrades a container to a newer image, BombVault can send a message per updated container so you can verify it still works. It fires only on a real update (rare) and is separate from the scheduled-run summary. Off by default. (#56)
- **Remove the old image after an update** (Settings → Storage → Image cleanup). After a post-backup update, optionally delete the superseded image. Off by default — keeping it makes rolling back cheap (a BombVault snapshot restores your data, not the old image), and a base image shared by other containers is never deleted. (#56)

Thanks to @BaukeZwart (#56).
